summary: All three attempts failed to resolve the same two SAST findings: codeql:py/path-injection (CWE-22) and bandit:B603 (CWE-78). The core issue is that user-supplied path validation occurs after file existence checks, allowing path traversal probes before the directory confinement check. The bandit finding is a false positive given shell=False usage, but the codeql findings remain true positives and were never addressed across attempts.
suggested_actions: Reorder validation: perform the directory confinement check (resolve().is_relative_to()) before any file existence or file-type checks on the user-supplied path.
Alternatively, resolve the path first and then validate all properties (existence, is_file, is_relative_to) using the resolved path to prevent path traversal probing.
For bandit:B603, add a comment or use a noqa directive to suppress the false positive, or restructure the subprocess call to be explicit about the safe usage.
Ensure that any path operations on user input are performed only after the path has been confirmed to be within the allowed directory.
